Financial clarity Strategic foresight Measurable impact
The e-commerce growth challenge

Scaling breaks finance first.

Rapid growth creates complexity faster than most finance setups can handle — and founders end up firefighting instead of growing.

  • Cash flow shocks from seasonality, platform fees, ad spend and inventory cycles
  • Financial data that arrives late, incomplete or unreliable
  • Stock decisions made on gut feel rather than unit economics
  • No clear line of sight on runway, margin or the next raise
82%of small businesses fail due to cash flow issues
65%of growing e-commerce businesses cite lack of real-time financial data as their biggest operational challenge
70%of online retailers struggle with inventory management
78%of successful scale-ups attribute their growth to strong financial controls and forecasting
How we deliver success

Solid foundations. Then finance becomes a competitive advantage.

Financial accounting sits at the core of everything we do. With a robust set of accounts in place, we overlay CFO and growth advisory that turns your numbers into decisions.

Key services

Everything a finance function should do — on demand.

“We scale our support as you grow.”

    Stress-test decisions before you make them.

  • Fundraising preparation & investor reporting

    Models, data rooms and reporting that stand up to diligence.

Results

Real brands. Real turnarounds.

Case study — turnaround

From 10 weeks of runway to sustained profitability

The challenge
Previous management had chased the growth dream — heavy spend on NPD, headcount, marketing and an expensive 3PL. The growth never materialised, significant debt was taken on, and runway was down to 10 weeks when we were appointed.
Our approach
Detailed cash flow forecasting and management. Forensic review of all spend: stock buys focused on fast-selling, higher-margin lines; selective price increases and reduced discounting; 25% negotiated reduction in 3PL costs; debt refinanced and trade finance introduced to shorten the cash conversion cycle.

Still trading 11 months on, with 5 consecutive months of EBITDA profitability — and a founder who can sleep at night.

Case study — scale-up

Unlocking growth a spreadsheet couldn’t see

The challenge
Incumbent accountants produced cash-accounted management accounts with no insight or guidance. The brand was consistently understocked despite a strong cash runway — driving poor reviews, subscription churn and chargebacks.
Our approach
Implemented detailed stock and cash flow planning, giving the founders the confidence to 4× stock purchases to meet demand. Restructured the supply chain to shorten the working capital cycle, restated accounts on an accrual basis and built a 3-year financial model.

Moving rapidly towards £30m ARR — highly profitable, cash generative, and on the way to an initial 7-figure equity raise.

Who we are

Operators, not observers.

We’ve sat in the CFO seat at the brands you’ve shopped with — building teams, raising money and steering high-velocity consumer businesses through scale.

NC

Nick Cooling

Co-Founder & Partner

Nick is a qualified management accountant (FCMA) whose career spans leading consumer and e-commerce brands including Warburtons, Magnet Kitchens and Boohoo.

As CFO of Online4baby he oversaw growth from £30m to £45m in 18 months, securing a £7.5m HSBC facility. As Finance Director of Cernucci he scaled the team from 12 to 40, raised £4.5m and drove revenue from £8m to £15m in two years.

LinkedIn ↗
KL

Karl Lloyd

Co-Founder & Partner

Karl qualified as a chartered accountant with EY before commercial finance roles at Matalan and The Co-op Group. As FD of ITC Luxury Travel he grew the business from £30m to £80m, completed two MBOs and sold to private equity.

He has been FD of scaling e-commerce brands including In The Style, Club L London, Lullabellz and Edge e-liquids — professionalising teams, implementing controls and building the models behind fundraises and exits.

Ryan C.

Partner

Ryan built his career in commercial finance at Boohoo Group, one of the UK’s largest online fashion retailers, working at the sharp end of trading, margin and performance analysis across high-velocity DTC brands.

He brings that commercial firepower to Praevo clients — turning trading data into pricing, stock and channel decisions that move the P&L.
